title: Explaining Favors
learningGoal: The learner will listen to a conversational dialogue to comprehend the exchange of physical items and the gratitude expressed through 'te-kureru'.
instructions: Listen to two friends talking about an umbrella. Try to catch who it belongs to and how the speaker got it.

contentItems.text: Listen for the verbs 'kuremashita' (gave me) and 'kashite kuremashita' (lent me).

listeningGoal: Identify the origin of the item and the favor that was done for the speaker.
audioScenario: Two colleagues chatting on a rainy day.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: これですか。ともだちが くれました。
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Kore desu ka. Tomodachi ga kuremashita.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: This? A friend gave it to me.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: そうですか。いいですね。
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Sou desu ka. Ii desu ne.

audioTranscriptSentences.targetText: はい。あめが ふったら、ともだちが かして くれました。
audioTranscriptSentences.transliteration: Hai. Ame ga futtara, tomodachi ga kashite kuremashita.
audioTranscriptSentences.baseTranslation: Yes. When it rained, my friend lent it to me.

audioTranscriptFull:
（同僚A）いい かさですね。
（同僚B）これですか。ともだちが くれました。
（同僚A）そうですか。いいですね。
（同僚B）はい。あめが ふったら、ともだちが かして くれました。

questionSet.questionText: What item are they talking about?
Who gave the item to the speaker?
Why did the friend give it to them?
Did the friend give the item permanently, or lend it?
Write the romaji for the phrase 'lent it to me' heard at the very end.

feedbackRules.onPass: Perfect listening! You successfully identified the favor.
feedbackRules.onFail: Listen again carefully. 'Kasa' = umbrella. 'Kashite kuremashita' = lent to me.
feedbackRules.onPartial: Good effort. Notice how the speaker first uses 'kuremashita' generally, then clarifies with 'kashite' (lend).

audioAssets.audioTranscript: いい かさですね。これですか。ともだちが くれました。そうですか。いいですね。はい。あめが ふったら、ともだちが かして くれました。

hintText: Listen for the verb 'Kasu' in its Te-form.
commonMistakes: Assuming the friend gave it permanently because of 'kuremashita' in line 2, but line 4 clarifies 'kashite' (lend).
